title: Kindle eBook
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Kindle and Mobipocket e-book files used on Amazon Kindle devices and apps. The `.mobi` extension covers the original Mobipocket format; `.azw` and `.azw3` are Amazon's proprietary variants with DRM and enhanced typography features.

## When to use
Use `media:mobi` when a pipeline node delivers or converts Kindle-targeted e-books — for example, a Calibre conversion step producing MOBI from EPUB, or a publishing pipeline that targets Kindle Direct Publishing.

## Notes
The AZW3 (Kindle Format 8) variant supports HTML5 and CSS3 features similar to EPUB 3, while older MOBI/AZW use a more limited markup subset. DRM-protected files cannot be read or converted without authorization keys. For open e-book formats, prefer [EPUB Document](https://capdag.com/browse/media:epub). See also [Kindle eBook](https://capdag.com/browse/media:mobi).
